message: Enforce sync JSONL allowlist and add opt-in flag

files.patch:
@@ -2,11 +2,11 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-0a5","title":"Feature: init Command Implementation","description":"# init Command Implementation

## Purpose
Initialize a beads workspace **without** any git hooks/merge drivers. Create `.beads/`, SQLite DB, metadata/config files, and apply schema migrations.

## CLI
```
br init [--prefix <prefix>] [--force] [--backend sqlite]
```

## Behavior (classic, non-invasive)
- Create `.beads/` directory.
- Create `.beads/beads.db` (or metadata.json `database` value).
- Apply schema/migrations.
- Write `.beads/metadata.json` (database + jsonl_export).
- Write `.beads/config.yaml` template (YAML-only keys).
- Write `.beads/.gitignore` with db/wal/shm and runtime files.
- **Do NOT** install git hooks, merge drivers, or git config.

## Prefix Resolution
Order:
1) `--prefix` flag
2) config `issue-prefix` (YAML)
3) first issue in JSONL history (if imported)
4) directory name
Normalize: store prefix **without** trailing `-`.

## Safety Guards
- Refuse if inside `.beads/` directory.
- If DB exists and `--force` not set, abort.
- If JSONL exists but DB missing, allow init (fresh clone path).

## Optional Import on Init
- If JSONL present, optionally import after init (non-fatal on errors).

## Output
- Text: `Initialized beads workspace in .beads/`
- JSON: `{ "status": "initialized", "path": ".beads/", "prefix": "bd" }`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Initializes DB + config files with no git operations.
- Prefix stored correctly.
- Schema compatibility verified.

## Tests
- Fresh init creates files.
- `--force` overwrites existing DB (with confirmation in CLI).
- Non-invasive: no git config changes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T01:14:10.186011804-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T03:50:41.823781652-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T03:50:41.823781652-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented init command logic in src/cli/commands/init.rs and integrated into main CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0a5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-g3i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T01:14:28.509934329-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0a5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-5xp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T01:14:28.973341898-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0a5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-59y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T01:14:29.016332554-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-0ol","title":"Phase 2: Core Commands - CRUD & Query Operations","description":"# Phase 2: Core Commands (CRUD + Query)

## Goals
Implement classic CRUD commands and core queries with full flag semantics and output parity.

## Deliverables
- `create`, `update`, `close`, `reopen`, `delete`
- `list`, `show`, `ready`, `blocked`
- Last-touched tracking
- Output formatting + JSON schema parity

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON output matches bd for classic commands.
- Text output passes golden snapshot tests.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T01:10:51.880242175-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T09:15:27.833749896-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T09:15:27.833749896-05:00","close_reason":"Completed Phase 2: Core Commands (CRUD + Query)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0ol","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-g3i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T01:11:14.909643612-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0ol","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-8f8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T01:11:15.145142096-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0ol","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-otn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:24:31.320314332-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0ol","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-k0y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:24:31.367665165-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0ol","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-hee","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:24:31.414165608-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0ol","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ykm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:24:31.512451485-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0ol","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-9od","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:24:31.560071476-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-0v1","title":"Sync safety hardening to prevent destructive repository changes in br","description":"Background: In another project, bd sync created commits that accidentally deleted all source files. This is catastrophic and unacceptable for br. br must be non-invasive and must never perform git operations or touch working-tree files outside .beads, except the explicit JSONL export path.\n\nGoal: Ensure br sync is provably safe, with hard guardrails, explicit user intent for any risky operations, atomic export/import, and a regression test suite that prevents any future recurrence.","acceptance_criteria":"- Sync paths are strictly constrained and validated\n- br performs no git operations, ever\n- Export/import are atomic and failure-safe\n- Unit, integration, and e2e tests prove no working-tree files are modified\n- E2E scripts capture detailed logs for postmortem analysis\n- Docs clearly state the safety model","notes":"This epic translates a real incident into concrete safeguards. The plan must be self-contained so future maintainers do not need to revisit the incident report. We will encode strict invariants, implementation guardrails, tests, and docs to make unsafe behavior impossible or loudly blocked.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:08.740121176-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:18:07.236205856-05:00"}
-{"id":"beads_rust-0v1.1","title":"Safety requirements and threat model for br sync","description":"Define the safety envelope for br sync based on the incident class where sync deleted all source files. Capture the threat model, failure modes, invariants, and explicit non-goals (no git operations, no daemon behavior, no auto-commit, no hooks).","acceptance_criteria":"- Written threat model with concrete failure scenarios\n- Explicit list of safety invariants and non-goals\n- Mapped mitigations for each scenario","notes":"This is the foundation for all implementation and tests. It should stand alone as a checklist of what must never happen, with rationale.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:14.424079184-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:14.424079184-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:14.42534169-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

Capture the threat model, failure modes, invariants, and explicit non-goals (no git operations, no daemon behavior, no auto-commit, no hooks).","acceptance_criteria":"- Written threat model with concrete failure scenarios\n- Explicit list of safety invariants and non-goals\n- Mapped mitigations for each scenario","notes":"This is the foundation for all implementation and tests. It should stand alone as a checklist of what must never happen, with rationale.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:14.424079184-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:27:20.703050445-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:27:20.703050445-05:00","close_reason":"All 4 children complete. Deliverables: SYNC_THREAT_MODEL.md (incident analysis, 6 failure scenarios, mitigations), SYNC_SAFETY_INVARIANTS.md (8 non-goals, 14 invariants), SYNC_CLI_FLAG_SEMANTICS.md (flag matrix, safe defaults)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:14.42534169-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.1","title":"Incident analysis and threat model for sync","description":"Capture the incident class: bd sync produced a commit that deleted all source files; recovery required git restore/reset. Analyze plausible root causes (overbroad path operations, unsafe git integration, auto-commit hooks, or mistaken repo root). Define threat actors: user error, unexpected file paths, corrupted JSONL, and tool bugs. Produce a threat model describing how br could accidentally touch non-.beads files and how to prevent it.","acceptance_criteria":"- Written analysis of incident class and root-cause categories\n- Threat model covers path traversal, git side effects, and partial writes\n- Each scenario maps to a specific mitigation","notes":"Threat model complete. See .beads/SYNC_THREAT_MODEL.md","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleFox","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:39.068634386-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:23:30.251387467-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:23:30.251387467-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:39.070002701-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.2","title":"Safety invariants and non-goals for br sync","description":"Define a precise, testable set of invariants for br sync. Examples: (1) No git commands executed. (2) No file creation/deletion outside the sync allowlist (.beads + explicit JSONL path only when user opts in). (3) JSONL export uses atomic write/rename and never truncates on failure. (4) Import refuses conflict markers and validates schema/prefix. (5) Any operation that could discard or override data requires explicit --force. (6) All sync decisions are logged at debug level with safe, non-sensitive detail.","acceptance_criteria":"- Invariants list is explicit, testable, and prioritized by risk\n- Non-goals are clearly stated (no git ops, no daemon, no hooks, no auto-commit)\n- Invariants map to guardrails and tests\n- Logging requirements are defined for safety-critical decisions","notes":"Safety invariants doc created at .beads/SYNC_SAFETY_INVARIANTS.md","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightMesa","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:44.608543467-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:24:11.522134329-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:24:11.522134329-05:00","close_reason":"Safety invariants documented: 8 non-goals, 14 testable invariants across 5 categories (path confinement, atomic writes, data loss prevention, input validation, no git ops), logging requirements, and test coverage matrix","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:44.610005188-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:05:35.430762171-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.2","title":"Safety invariants and non-goals for br sync","description":"Define a precise, testable set of invariants for br sync. Examples: (1) No git commands executed. (2) No file creation/deletion outside the sync allowlist (.beads + explicit JSONL path only when user opts in). (3) JSONL export uses atomic write/rename and never truncates on failure. (4) Import refuses conflict markers and validates schema/prefix. (5) Any operation that could discard or override data requires explicit --force. (6) All sync decisions are logged at debug level with safe, non-sensitive detail.","acceptance_criteria":"- Invariants list is explicit, testable, and prioritized by risk\n- Non-goals are clearly stated (no git ops, no daemon, no hooks, no auto-commit)\n- Invariants map to guardrails and tests\n- Logging requirements are defined for safety-critical decisions","notes":"Safety invariants doc created at .beads/SYNC_SAFETY_INVARIANTS.md","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightMesa","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:44.608543467-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:27:03.2184278-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:27:03.2184278-05:00","close_reason":"Safety invariants documented in .beads/SYNC_SAFETY_INVARIANTS.md with risk prioritization (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), explicit non-goals (NG-1 through NG-8), invariant-to-guard mapping, and logging requirements. Document meets all acceptance criteria.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:44.610005188-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:05:35.430762171-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.3","title":"Sync safety spec with acceptance mapping","description":"Write a short spec that translates invariants into concrete behavior for export/import, preflight, error messages, and CLI flags. Include acceptance mapping to tests (unit/integration/e2e) and logging expectations for each safety decision.","acceptance_criteria":"- Spec defines expected behavior for export/import and failure cases\n- Each invariant has a corresponding test plan entry (unit/integration/e2e)\n- CLI/UX implications and explicit opt-ins are captured\n- Logging expectations are listed for each safety check","notes":"This is the handoff document for implementation. Keep it tight and actionable, but include enough detail to implement tests without guesswork.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:50.027842209-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:25:03.926412219-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:25:03.926412219-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:50.029165148-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:05:35.48437072-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.4","title":"Define safe CLI flag semantics and user-intent gating","description":"Specify which operations require explicit flags (e.g., --force, --import-only, --flush-only), and define default-safe behavior. Define explicit opt-in for any external JSONL path or non-.beads location (including BEADS_JSONL). Ensure br never performs auto-sync or git operations by default. Clarify how --no-auto-* flags should behave in br.","acceptance_criteria":"- Clear flag matrix for sync behaviors\n- Defaults are safe and non-invasive\n- External JSONL paths require explicit opt-in\n- Any risky operation requires explicit user intent","notes":"Avoid implicit behavior that could surprise users or modify repo state. Include examples for safe and unsafe invocations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:54.478553224-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:26:06.814049505-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:26:06.814049505-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:54.48068715-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:05:35.537515275-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

Define explicit opt-in for any external JSONL path or non-.beads location (including BEADS_JSONL). Ensure br never performs auto-sync or git operations by default. Clarify how --no-auto-* flags should behave in br.","acceptance_criteria":"- Clear flag matrix for sync behaviors\n- Defaults are safe and non-invasive\n- External JSONL paths require explicit opt-in\n- Any risky operation requires explicit user intent","notes":"CLI flag semantics doc created at .beads/SYNC_CLI_FLAG_SEMANTICS.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:54.478553224-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:27:08.160145337-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:27:08.160145337-05:00","close_reason":"CLI flag semantics documented: flag matrix, safety guard bypass rules, external path handling, error policies, safe/unsafe invocation examples, and help message templates","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:54.48068715-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:05:35.537515275-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.2","title":"Sync guardrails implementation (path, atomicity, safety checks)","description":"Implement the core safety guardrails in br sync: strict path allowlist, canonicalization, atomic export/import, conflict-marker detection, and explicit user-intent gating for any potentially risky behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"- Path validation and allowlist enforced for sync IO\n- Export/import are atomic and failure-safe\n- Risky operations require explicit flags\n- Preflight checks run before any writes\n- Safety checks and decisions are logged","notes":"All code changes must be driven by the safety spec. Prefer small, auditable helpers with unit tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:19.686594125-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:19:03.127843629-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:19.687861249-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.1","title":"Implement path allowlist and canonicalization for sync IO","description":"Add a centralized path-validation helper that enforces: sync IO paths must reside under the active .beads directory OR be an explicitly allowed JSONL path (requires opt-in flag); must not escape via .. or symlinks; must never target repository source files. Helper is used by export, import, preflight, and any temp-file cleanup.","acceptance_criteria":"- Path validation helper exists and is used in sync export/import/preflight\n- Attempts to use paths outside the allowlist fail fast with clear errors\n- Symlink, traversal, and absolute-path edge cases are handled\n- Opt-in for external JSONL paths is enforced","notes":"Primary guardrail against overbroad path operations. Use canonicalized paths and explicit allowlist checks; avoid TOCTOU by validating parent directories and creating temp files in the target dir.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:04:00.545619633-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:16:43.455783547-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:04:00.547737047-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:05:42.42323102-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:06:39.850049204-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.2","title":"Make JSONL export atomic and failure-safe","description":"Ensure export writes to a temp file in the same directory, fsyncs, and atomically renames into place. Never truncate or overwrite the existing JSONL on failure. Preserve file permissions where possible and log each phase at debug level.","acceptance_criteria":"- Export uses write-to-temp + fsync + atomic rename\n- Existing JSONL is preserved on failure\n- Temp files are cleaned up safely within allowlist\n- Logs show export phases and final outcome","notes":"Atomic export prevents partial writes and data loss; detailed logs support postmortems without exposing sensitive data.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:04:04.858265362-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:16:47.79429024-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:04:04.859911339-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:05:42.474522448-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:05:42.523650556-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
@@ -15,7 +15,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.5","title":"Add explicit guards against filesystem deletion outside .beads","description":"Ensure sync never deletes any filesystem content outside the allowed .beads paths. If deletion is ever needed (e.g., temp file cleanup), it must be scoped and validated. Add a centralized guard that rejects any delete/overwrite targeting repo source files, and log attempted unsafe paths.","acceptance_criteria":"- Delete/overwrite operations are scoped to allowed paths\n- Any attempt to target other paths fails fast and logs the rejection\n- Guard is reused across sync components","notes":"Even temporary cleanup can be dangerous if paths are wrong. Make deletion impossible outside the allowed scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:04:21.54983608-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:17:02.775020641-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:04:21.551839409-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:05:42.778462162-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.6","title":"Add sync safety checks to br doctor","description":"Extend br doctor to validate sync safety preconditions: JSONL path within allowlist, no merge conflict markers, metadata consistency, and absence of unsafe config (e.g., external JSONL without opt-in). Provide clear guidance if checks fail. Log diagnostics in verbose mode.","acceptance_criteria":"- Doctor reports unsafe JSONL paths and conflict markers\n- Doctor flags unsafe config or missing opt-in for external paths\n- Doctor offers actionable remediation steps\n- Checks are read-only","notes":"Doctor is the safe entry point for users; it should prevent risky sync operations before they run.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:04:26.679333733-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:17:07.634879094-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.6","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:04:26.68067653-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.6","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:05:42.827597273-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.6","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:05:42.879567148-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.7","title":"Add sync preflight stage with explicit safety checks","description":"Introduce a preflight step for sync that validates workspace state (beads dir exists, JSONL path safe, metadata consistent, no conflict markers if importing) before any writes occur. Preflight must be read-only, fail-fast, and log decision points at debug level.","acceptance_criteria":"- Preflight runs before export/import\n- Preflight is read-only and fail-fast\n- Preflight errors are actionable\n- Logs show which checks ran and what failed","notes":"Preflight reduces the risk of partial operations and makes the safety model explicit.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:06:14.416975074-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:17:12.471677868-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.7","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:06:14.418964979-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.7","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:06:39.697459506-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.7","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:06:39.750990328-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.2.9","title":"Add structured logging around sync safety decisions","description":"Instrument sync with structured tracing spans and debug logs for preflight checks, path allowlist decisions, export/import phases, and safety rejections. Ensure logs are detailed enough for postmortems but do not leak sensitive content.  {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.3","title":"Safety test suite and regression coverage for sync","description":"Build a rigorous test suite that proves sync cannot modify repository source files, cannot invoke git, and is atomic/failure-safe. Include unit, integration, and end-to-end scenarios that simulate real repos.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:23.753646355-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:18:59.177599649-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-0v1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0v1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:03:23.755023316-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.3.1","title":"Unit tests: path guard, conflict markers, atomic export","description":"Add unit tests for the path allowlist/canonicalization helper, conflict-marker detection, and atomic export behavior (temp file -> rename).  {"id":"beads_rust-0v1.4","title":"Docs and operational guidance for safe sync","description":"Document the sync safety model, explicit user-intent requirements, and non-goals (no git ops).
